pi_items.source: Confirmed via export_result.sh which explicitly checks CSV_PATH='/home/ga/RProjects/output/fpca_variance.csv'
Confirmed via export_result.sh which explicitly checks MEAN_PNG='/home/ga/RProjects/output/mean_growth_curve.png'
Confirmed via export_result.sh which explicitly checks PC1_PNG='/home/ga/RProjects/output/pc1_variation.png'
Confirmed via export_result.sh which explicitly checks SCRIPT_PATH='/home/ga/RProjects/growth_analysis.R'

Calculated empirical PC1 variance on the 'fda' R package 'growth' dataset's female cohort, which yielded ~0.89. This correctly falls inside the required evaluation bound [0.80, 0.95] specified in export_result.sh.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real-world longitudinal height data from the Berkeley Growth Study, supplied via the 'fda' R package ('growth' dataset). I verified that calculating the first principal component (PC1) variance for the female height cohort—using standard B-spline smoothing or un-smoothed raw PCA—yields approximately 0.88-0.89, which correctly falls within the task's required bounding box of [0.80, 0.95]. Output file paths and expectations exactly match the evaluation scripts.
